text.text:
We stopped by this dessert place and it was just okay overall. The cake itself was nice and tasted good, but the prices are really hard to justify. £10 for a slice of cake feels quite crazy, and even the cheapest coffee was £5, which I’ve honestly never seen before.

Also, there is another shop in Soho selling the same cakes, so it doesn’t really feel unique enough to justify those prices. Nothing was bad, but for what you pay we expected something much more special. Because of that, it’s probably not somewhere we would go back to

1st: the pictures you will take are better than the food by around 3x. The food is okay, it’s not bad but surely not outstanding.

2nd: the service was actively bad.

We arrived and they started a countdown - that we already knew, but coming to the table every 2 minutes doesn’t help.
They didn’t bring a spoon for the cappuccino, I asked, nothing came for almost 10 minutes. One spoon for a cappuccino that was already there took ten minutes when the pressure to order was VERY MUCH present every 2 minutes (and yes I timed it).
3rd: food wise, disappointing.
Being rushed, I ended up ordering the Rich and Rustic and it roughly does the job. The bread is a bit sad, the rest is just a but unseasoned.
The cappuccino is not as bad as a machine one but not nearly what is supposed to be.

Well, you’re not gonna starve but it is just not worth the money.

text.text:
What a place. Not my usual haunt, but when meeting a new client in a new area one must take risks….boy am I glad I did!

Coffee was excellent ( photo included ) , it’s rare to find a Barista that knows their craft well enough to make such a solid flat white. Tasted good too - in fact I came back for a second.

Josh was exemplary - despite it being what I can only assume their busy hour, Josh was beaming from ear to ear and friendly whilst pumping our beveraginos. Like Jesus turning water into wine, Josh effortlessly turns beans & milk into a lovely little coffee. I divulge - he was friendly and amicable too, remaining genuine and not overdoing it.

There was one other chap working, although we did not interact directly he seemed to be cut from a similar cloth to the aforementioned Josh. I didn’t have my glasses but I believe his name tag said Harry

Great coffee and Great staff - management , if you’re reading this it’s chaps like Josh that keep us punters coming back!

text.text:
As a coffee enthusiast, I was blown away by Kiss the Hippo. The single origin flat white  was outstanding. Fruity, well balanced, and topped with beautiful latte art that showed real care and skill. Every sip felt vibrant and complex without being overwhelming.

We also tried the cinnamon bun, which was fantastic: wonderfully fluffy and light, never dry. Personally, I would have liked just a touch more sweetness, but that’s purely a personal preference.

The interior is gorgeous, with a clean, modern design that feels both stylish and welcoming. A must-visit for anyone serious about great coffee.

The place is so cool, but the food is even better. I walked in with my cousin without a reservation, and we waited less than 20 minutes to be seated.
It was my cousin’s first time in London, so I wanted her to try the American pancakes with Nutella on the side and the açaí bowl—she absolutely loved them. I ordered my favourite avocado on toast, and it was delicious.
For drinks, we had a mango matcha iced latte (I chose oat milk) and an Ombré Passion Fruit Cooler—so fresh, you can really taste the fruit. We also tried the Christmas special dessert, the Snow Place Bauble Cake. The table looked magical and beautifully presented. Everything was worth it—every dish tasted perfect. Stuff so helpful and nice even when was very busy .🥰

text.text:
I’ve always associated EL&N with impeccable aesthetics and the polished visual experience they showcase on Instagram. That’s why walking into this branch today was genuinely shocking the space looks nothing like the standard the brand claims to represent.

The ambience, which is supposed to be EL&N’s strongest point, was visibly deteriorated. The sofas were torn, peeling and with the foam fully exposed, looking more like discarded furniture than seating in a premium café. The chairs were in the same condition dirty, cracked and falling apart. None of this reflects the “instagrammable” identity the brand markets so heavily.

The floor was dirty, stained and clearly not cleaned recently. The counter displaying the cakes had dark marks, accumulated grime and exposed cardboard boxes, completely breaking the illusion of care. Even worse, there was a regular bin placed right next to the food, with the orange bin liner fully visible highly unappealing and questionable from a hygiene perspective.

The ceiling had holes, loose panels, cracked paint and exposed structures, giving the entire space an abandoned feel. The service area behind the counter looked disorganised, with stained shelves and utensils on display. The bathroom had an unpleasant smell, reinforcing the sense of poor maintenance.

Nothing about this visit reflected the visual identity EL&N is known for. The environment was so uncomfortable that I couldn’t bring myself to eat. It felt like a place without management, without maintenance and entirely inconsistent with the brand’s promise.

I sincerely hope this branch receives immediate attention, because the aesthetics the very thing that makes EL&N what it is were simply not present today.

It was my first time visiting EL&N Café in London, and I was really excited to finally try their famous milk cake. It was good, but honestly not as good as some of their competition (I’m sure many of you already know exactly who I’m talking about!). Still, it was enjoyable and worth trying at least once.

What really stood out to me was the café’s ambience. The whole place is colorful, vibrant, and beautifully designed — definitely one of the most Instagrammable cafés in London. The atmosphere is fun, lively, and perfect for anyone who loves aesthetic places. Overall, it’s a nice spot to visit, especially for the experience and the décor.

It is good to support a long standing, independent business over the nearby chains selling inferior coffee that is virtually untraceable.

The shop is just a short walk from TCR or Piccadilly stations.

text.text:
Come backing again (2 Oct 2025), my last day in London. This time I came here via Smith's Court.  I tried their cortado, beautifully made with latte art stayed intact. This is definitely a testament to skill -  the dense of the coffee, the right temperature to steam the milk, of course the coffee beans, all play a critical role.  Their croissant was good, though it would be even better offer a heatup service.  I also saw their cold brew is readily available in the fridge when I lined up the queue,  which I appreciates as I always enjoy cold brew without ice.

2nd time visit on 1 Oct 2025 for quick lunch. I ordered a cold crew and almond apricot pastry. Both are lovely. This is the best spot in the area.

1st time visit, 14 Sept 2025. I ordered the long black and it was excellent. The cafe had a very chill vibe, especially with Westerman's music playing during my visit.

This is the good place to have a snack before going to watch the musical.

text.text:
Scarlett Green was a wonderful place to get breakfast/brunch. Seating was comfortable and abundant though I would recommend a reservation as it does fill up quickly.

The Smoked Salmon Royale was a very satisfying meal with quality ingredients that left me satiated. Tender salmon, perfectly poached eggs and a delicious hollandaise. The chili pesto was mild so if you like spicy I suggest getting either more or asking for a hot sauce.

Overall service was swift and the ambiance was inviting. Given the opportunity I would dine-in again to try more of the varied menu.

text.text:
We chose Scarlett Green for my birthday brunch and had a nicetime. The location is very convenient in central London.

The atmosphere is great – a mix of industrial style with lots of greenery, creating a spacious and vibrant setting across two floors. It was quite busy during our visit, so the service was a little on the slower side, but the staff were friendly.

I had the royal eggs with salmon they were okay, though I was expecting a slightly larger portion and the toast was a bit hard. We also tried the pancakes, which were incredibly fluffy but a bit too sweet for my taste. There’s a nice variety of drinks available; I had a Bellong cocktail which was really enjoyable. The highlight was definitely the surprise birthday cake from the team – such a lovely touch!

Overall, Scarlett Green is a great spot for brunch with a fun atmosphere.

text.text:
Top-notch.
I've tried many coffees from all over London, and this is one of the few places I want to return to and buy another cup. Inside, the cafe is far from elegant and luxurious, but it's a masterpiece of coffee brewing.
They use their own brand of beans: a blend from Brazil, Colombia, and Rwanda.
Flavors of milk chocolate, pecans, raisins, and dried fruit.
After the first sip, you can tell this coffee is of exceptional quality and taste.
A 6oz  flat white for £3.80 is a good price.
On a scale of 1 to 10, I give it a 9.
Bravo!!!

text.text:
We went there with my partner and our dog. We had to wait around 20 minutes to get in, as the place was absolutely packed, but it was totally worth it. Once inside, we had such a fantastic experience.
Even though it was incredibly busy, every single member of staff was smiling, cheerful, and genuinely kind. You couldn’t feel any stress at all, even though they were clearly working nonstop. They immediately made us feel welcome, and they were so lovely with our dog, giving her cuddles and making sure we all felt comfortable from the start.
The service was fast, the food was delicious, and the place itself is really charming. After so many years living in London, we’ve noticed how hospitality standards have sadly gone down in recent years, but this place truly stands out. It reminded us what great service and genuine warmth feel like.
Highly recommend it, great food, great atmosphere, and truly wonderful people.

text.text:
When I learned that there was a coffee shop just off the beaten path in Soho that just so happens to share the name with my absolute favorite coffee drink I had to check it out.

I arrived 10min before close, so, I wasn't able to really sit in the ambiance that is Flat White. That being said, the staff was friendly, and the service was quick.

Before I talk about the drink experience, some additional details... I fell in love with flat whites when I visited Australia several years ago and I've been ordering and making them at home ever since. One thing worth noting is that a UK (and much of Europe) flat white is not quite the same as an Aussie or American flat white. It's still the same components; rich and bold espresso shot(s) accompanied by textured milk (steam milk infused with air to create a velvety texture), but in the UK they are restricted to cup sizes for some reason (mostly to do with the ratio of espresso to cup size). I was presented with a 4oz to-go coffee cup. The flavor was great, and with execution solid.

While this particular drink was delicious, I think I still prefer an Aussie flat white (or American). Having the barely bigger than espresso size feels like the drink is condensed while I want to enjoy the drink and flavor for longer.

We’ve seen this place soo many times whilst in London, and it’s always packed out. And the queue across the road is usually massive.

Given the queue we’ve always been put off. However, this Christmas Eve, we decided we’d wait in the queue to finally give it a shot.

We were told the wait is 30 minutes, so we queued across the road. However, we only had to wait 15 minutes.

We ended up getting a bench seat at the window.

We ordered their hot chocolates, and opted for the dark and milk mix,as we were advised by the staff that it tastes richer and nicer. We also got one of their chocolate cakes.

The hot chocolate was really nice, it was rich and very chocolatey, alongside the cake - jt was definitely chocolate overload. Not complaining it was really nice to treat ourselves.

Best hot chocolate I’ve had.

text.text:
First time trying Crème and honestly I get the hype. Entering the store, I was graciously greeted by every single employee there. I told them I was interested in trying a cookie, and they went through every cookie on display. The dark triple chocolate & seasalt is the one I purchased and there were no regrets. Crispy edges, gooey center — basically everything my therapist told me not to fall for. £5 for a cookie is exorbitant, but it is a hefty cookie with a lot of ingredients that should only be purchased for a treat occasionally, in my opinion.

A few tips- try going closer to opening hours. I went on a Saturday around 10am and the store was empty. Going during busier times, I don't think the experience would be as appreciable. Also, I highly recommend walking a few minutes to Soho Square Garden to enjoy your dessert.

A few tips- try going closer to opening hours. I went on a Saturday around 10am and the store was empty. Going during busier times, I don't think the experience would be as appreciable. Also, I highly recommend walking a few minutes to Soho Square Garden to enjoy your dessert.

editorialSummary.text: Store specialising in American-style home baking, including cupcakes, pies & cheesecakes.

text.text:
Extremely overpriced, tasteless, and clearly not fresh.

I bought cupcakes from the Soho branch on a Saturday afternoon, expecting freshness — but what I got was the complete opposite. The cupcakes were dry and stale, which was obvious the moment I bit into one and cut into it.

The woman in the shop mentioned they can last up to three days, and honestly, these tasted well past that point. For the prices they charge (£21.75 for 6!), that’s completely unacceptable. You’d expect same-day freshness, especially on a busy weekend, although the shop was a ghost town so I wouldn’t be surprised if nothing was fresh.

Massively disappointing experience and awful cupcakes. Lola’s Cupcakes has never let me down, but Hummingbird absolutely has. I can only imagine their days are numbered if this is the standard they’re offering.

Would not recommend. Stay away.

Panadera is definitely a pure morning magic. It's the perfect spot before starting your day or after the run. The Leche Flan Danish alone is worth setting an alarm and queuing up for: it's so buttery, and definitely melts like crack. Hands down also the best sando in town with their corned beef hash sando: the best. Savoury, juicy, perfectly seasoned

Also please go for breakfast (the earlier you go, the more goodies you'll get), trust me: the milo mocha paired with their breakfast sando is an elite combo - exactly what a perfect recipe for the best morning of your life should taste like.

text.text:
We were on our way to hop on the hop on hop off bus when we randomly came across this bagel bakery and I am really glad we stopped. I am honestly not a big bagel person but this place completely surprised me. We came with some family and ordered a plain bagel and a smoked salmon bagel and both were really good.

Everything tasted very fresh from the bagels to the ingredients. The texture was perfect and the smoked salmon bagel was flavorful without being too heavy. You can tell a lot of care goes into the food here and I can see why this place has such high ratings.

The atmosphere was also really nice. We visited in December and it was very cold outside but they had heaters set up which made it comfortable to sit and enjoy our food without feeling rushed. It made the experience much better.

Customer service was top notch. Everyone was friendly welcoming and patient and made us feel appreciated as customers. That always makes a big difference especially when traveling.

Overall this was a great find and a very pleasant surprise. I would definitely come back again and would recommend this place to anyone in the area whether you are a big bagel fan or just looking for a good spot to grab something fresh and tasty.

Large bakery, wide selection of freshly baked goods and always very busy. The pandan sponge cake was soft and fluffy, but flavour-wise a bit shy. The handmade milk tea (less sugar) also wasn’t really our cup of tea — literally.

text.text:
An extremely popular bakery in Chinatown, minutes away from Leicester Square tube station. Compared to other bakeries, items are competitively priced. Both sweet and savoury items and a variety that cannot be compared with. Items disappear from the shelves quickly and staff struggle to keep up with filling the shelves. Weekends are perpetually crowded which shows the popularity of the place. My personal favourites are the Coconut bun, peanutbutter bun and the chicken bun.
Once you have tasted the bakery treats, you will surely come back.

This bakery sells fresh Chinese bakery products. They taste delicious. It is very small inside. On the left of the shop, you walk in and take a tray and a set of tongs, then you walk around and  take your items from the shelves and pop it on your tray.
Then you walk into the next room, queue up and pay.
You can also get freshly cooked fish moulded bakery custard bites, and ice cream in the summer.

We strolled through London to find an interesting coffee and pastry option for the morning. We definitely found exactly what we were looking for. Cafe de Nata on Old Compton Street in Soho, London. Their Portuguese custard pastries are delicious. Priced very reasonably, well balanced between sweet and special flavors, and baked right in location. They are delicious! Make sure you stop by when you are in the area.

Hidden gem in the heart of Soho! ✨
I stopped by Café de Nata and ordered the raspberry nata — it was fresh, creamy, and delicious! 🍓 The pastry was warm, flaky, and perfectly balanced — definitely worth every bite.

I love that the shop stays open late — such a rare find in central London! The staff were super friendly and welcoming, making the whole experience even better. 💛

As a Google Local Guide who loves discovering dog-friendly, cozy cafés around the city, I’m always happy to share gems like this. Can’t wait to come back for the classic egg tart and maybe try some new flavors next time! 🥰

text.text:
I’ve been lucky to have eaten some amazing pastel de nata in Lisbon. These Portuguese egg tarts have taken off in London

It’s interesting that the English, the Chinese and Portuguese all have their own version of the egg tart with each culture adding their little twist

At Cafe de Nata, they’ve added another additional twist with fruit flavours. I’m a traditionalist and prefer the original version. A great egg tart but ooomph £3 for a little egg tart. No wonder there’s a cost of living crisis

text.text: People say this cafe serves delicious, freshly baked pastel de nata with a variety of flavors, including classic, chocolate, raspberry, and vegan options. They highlight the cozy atmosphere and the fast service. They also like the friendly staff.

I saw Cocomelts cookies pop up on Instagram and had to try them. I was having a night in, so ordered them on Deliveroo. We ordered the chocolate chip and triple chocolate.

They were very good. The best cookies I've had in London so far. My personal preference is a bit fluffier, but these are pretty flawless. I did find the triple chocolate a bit too sweet. But again, that's just me. The chocolate chip was very well balanced.

We did not need the chocolate dip, they were perfectly fine. My friend took one home, reheated it and said it was even better warm.

I'm not sure why they are a 4.2? But, I guess I've only tried the cookies. I would certainly order again and recommend trying it for yourself. I'm usually skeptical if Google reviews are below a 4.4, but, seeing the reel on Instagram gave me faith.

**Update** we also had them in, they were incredible. I've not had a better cookie in London.

I would give it a higher rating if it weren't for the service.
I've been here twice now with my husband (late both times - around 10.44pm).
The first time [although it was close to closing time] they were far more accommodating, than the second time.
The second time ...not so much (and for the price point It was really off-putting) and I highly doubt I'll ever be going back.
It closes at 11pm so the initial time they allowed us to sit in with a takeaway container which was fair. But although their hot chocolate was nice, my banana crepe was divine, and my husband loved the strawberries with melted chocolate- it wasn't anything you couldn't make at home...
So for the poor customer service (I'm sorry) I wouldn't recommend spending your money to come here.

It seems everything is made fresh on-site and it shows.

The quality of service is excellent as well.

The interior isn’t fancy, but it is comfortable (imho) and I’ve enjoyed leisurely mornings drinking my tea and enjoying my scone.

I’ll plan on coming here any time I’m staying nearby. It’s wonderful.

text.text:
Like so many others, we saw these amazing desserts were trending on social media. And ofcourse, the teenager of our home really wanted to try one of the cute rabbit themed desserts.
Hidden down a beautiful street, we easily found the cafe.
We found the mousses on display in the window, with a brief description of their flavours. I must say, they were every bit as perfect as you see online, and were very, very unique. I can see why they appeal to so many people!
We made our selection, choosing three small mousses, and chose a brown sugar boba milk tea, and decided to eat on the premises. The price for the 3 desserts (no bigger than a rich tea or digestive biscuit - as those biscuits were the base for the mousse) and the 2 boba teas came in at a whopping £39!
Which we felt was expensive, even for London prices!
The cafe itself was OK.... but lacked the wow factor. The walls were unpainted plaster, so had that patchy peach colour. The bench seating was slightly uncomfortable, but did have small round pads to sit on. They just weren't very cushioned!
The desserts were rich but pleasant.
Although I mentioned earlier that the size of them were fairly small, I think they were actually the perfect size, bearing in mind the richness of them.
You certainly couldn't eat a large amount of them, even with a sweet tooth!
But overall, it is somewhere I would recommend trying, mainly for the memories. It isn't the kind of place I would want to go back to. And I think that's probably their general custom. It probably appeals to Londons tourists, those who want to get a cute photo for their social media.
The service was OK. We ordered as we entered, and apart from the staff bringing the food and drinks over, we didn't interact with them any further.
They didnt come and ask if things were OK etc, but we didn't mind that.
There wasn't really an atmosphere of any kind there. I think that they could add some cute decor in theme to their desserts.
As I would imagine that most visitors do take photos for their platforms.
With a little more decor, like themed photo walls, this could create a better vibe.
It was empty when we went in. Only one other table was occupied, a couple with their dog.
That's another thing..... we saw someone walk in, and decide to leave because they had an allergy to dogs.
We actually loved fussing over the dog, but understand not everyone would like a dog near their food!
Overall.... Great for a once only visit. But not somewhere we would go back to.
Photos included to further explain our review. Hope it helps someone 🥰

text.text:
Tsujiri Chinatown presents itself as a distinctive culinary destination that specialises in authentic matcha-based beverages and desserts. The quality of the products is noteworthy, with the drinks offering a balanced richness and the desserts showcasing a refined taste that highlights the versatility of matcha. This emphasis on flavour authenticity contributes to a memorable gastronomic experience, particularly for individuals with an appreciation for Japanese tea culture.

The location proves highly convenient, situated within the vibrant Chinatown district where foot traffic and transport links enhance its appeal. This ease of access strengthens its suitability as both a casual stop for refreshment and a social gathering point.

However, a critical consideration relates to the pricing structure, which is positioned at a slightly higher level than comparable establishments. While the premium reflects the quality and brand reputation, it may influence its accessibility to a wider demographic of consumers.

Overall, Tsujiri Chinatown succeeds in delivering high-quality matcha drinks and desserts within a strategically convenient setting. The slightly elevated cost remains its primary limitation, but this is mitigated by the unique cultural and sensory value it offers.

text.text:
If you are a true coffee connoisseur, this Japanese specialty coffee shop is a dream come true. Unlike many places that try to do everything, this cafe focuses strictly on the art of coffee, and the results are extraordinary.
I tried their V60 pour-over using a light-roast Japanese bean, and the clarity of flavor was mind-blowing. The precision the baristas use—from the water temperature to the pouring technique—is a testament to the Japanese dedication to perfection. It’s not just a caffeine fix; it’s a craft.
The space itself is minimalist and serene, designed to let you focus entirely on the aroma and taste of your brew. No distractions, just high-quality beans and expert preparation. It’s easily one of the best spots in London for anyone who appreciates the subtle notes of a well-brewed cup.
The staff are incredibly knowledgeable and passionate about their craft. If you take your coffee seriously, you need to visit this place. A truly refined and authentic experience!

text.text:
Onsu Patisserie
London & Soho really needed this — I absolutely loved it.

Onsu is clean, precise, Asian-influenced pastry with strong Western foundations, properly executed. The pastries are beautifully crafted, well balanced, with great textures and flavour harmony. Nothing heavy, nothing overdone — just disciplined, confident work.
The wagyu curry bun was seriously tasty. The mushroom truffle toast was delicious. And the matcha… honestly the best I’ve had since I was in Japan and Korea. Proper high-quality matcha — what a difference from the standard chain versions.
At the centre is Michael Kwan — European Pastry Cup Champion and 4th place at the World Pastry Cup. With a Michelin-star background and five-star hotel experience, that standard runs through the whole place.
The team have worked together for years, many of them competition winners themselves. I had the pleasure of seeing the kitchen — busy, focused and very professional. You can feel the passion.
If I lived around the corner, I’d be here daily.

text.text:
I recently visited the Boba Bubble Tea store in Chinatown, and unfortunately, it turned out to be one of the most disappointing experiences I’ve had in a long time. I was really looking forward to having a nice refreshing drink, but both of the teas I ordered were completely tasteless and not worth the money at all. I ordered one Milk Bubble Tea and one Passionfruit Fruit Tea, spending around £13 in total, and honestly, it felt like I just threw that money away. The Milk Bubble Tea tasted just like plain water there was no creaminess, no tea flavour, and even the tapioca pearls didn’t help. It lacked that rich, smooth taste that a good milk tea should have. It was so bland that I couldn’t even finish half of it. The Passionfruit Fruit Tea was just as bad. It literally tasted like water with a faint, artificial hint of something sweet, but definitely not passionfruit. It was flat, watered down, and had zero real fruit flavour. I took a couple of sips and left both drinks on the table because they were honestly undrinkable.
I really hate wasting food or drinks, but this time, there was just no point in forcing myself to finish them. The only positive part of the visit was the staff they were friendly, polite, and tried their best to be helpful. It’s not their fault that the drinks were so disappointing, but the quality definitely needs major improvement.
Overall, it was a total waste of money, and I regret choosing this place. For £13, I expected something enjoyable, but instead I got two cups of flavoured water. Definitely not recommended.

text.text:
If you’re in London and craving the real deal in bubble tea, Xing Fu Tangon Frith Street in Soho is a must-visit. As the original Taiwanese brand that sparked the global brown sugar boba craze, they’ve brought their signature craftsmanship straight to the heart of the city—and it does not disappoint.

I tried their iconic Signature Brown Sugar Boba Fresh Milk (also listed as “焰遇幸福招牌黑糖珍珠鮮奶” on the menu), and wow—this isn’t just another sweet drink. The tapioca pearls are hand-stirred and freshly cooked in-house giving them an incredible chewy texture and rich caramelized brown sugar flavor that’s deep but not cloying . What really sets it apart is how they coat the inside of the cup with warm brown sugar and pearls creating that Instagram-famous “tiger stripe” effect—equal parts art and indulgence .

The fresh milk is smooth and balances the sweetness perfectly. I went with standard sugar (which is actually quite moderate) and found it just right—no artificial aftertaste, just pure, comforting warmth in a cup.

Located at 29 Frith Street, Soho (W1D 5LG) the shop is tiny but vibrant, often with a short queue—proof of its popularity . While they offer other drinks like Biscoff boba and fruit teas, the brown sugar boba milk remains the star . Pro tip: go early! They sometimes sell out of pearls by late afternoon .

As someone who’s tried boba from Taipei to NYC, I can confidently say Xing Fu Tang in London delivers an authentic, high-quality experience that bubble tea lovers shouldn’t miss. It’s more than a drink—it’s a moment of pure, chewy, caramel-kissed joy. 💛

Highly recommend for students, tourists, or anyone needing a little “xing fu” (happiness) in their day!

text.text:
The Osmanthus Experiment That Passed with Flying Colours

Today’s field test at Cha Halo London: Osmanthus Delight.
Hypothesis: A floral–tea fusion could deliver both comfort and character.
Procedure: One freshly brewed cup, observed under normal café conditions.
Results: The osmanthus aroma released slowly, like opening a warm greenhouse door. On first sip, the tea base carried a smooth, almost velvety texture, with just the right hint of natural sweetness – no artificial aftertaste detected. The floral notes lingered, layering over each breath like a quiet echo.
Conclusion: Hypothesis confirmed. This isn’t just a drink – it’s a well-engineered sensory experiment. No wonder it’s their bestseller.

I’ve been a customer of Kissaten since it first opened inside the laundromat. Over the years, I’ve watched it grow in popularity, but unfortunately, the quality of service has declined. Today was the first time I left genuinely frustrated.

The shop is now overcrowded with gacha machines, leaving very little space to stand or move comfortably. A single small service window now handles both gacha and drink orders, resulting in long delays—especially when someone places a large gacha order. During my visit, around ten drink orders appeared to be stalled with no visible progress. Several customers voiced their concerns, but the staff seemed largely indifferent.

After waiting for about 15 minutes, we were informed that our drink was unavailable—something that should have been communicated at the time of ordering. No apology or compensation was offered. When we asked for a refund and expressed our frustration, the staff member told us we were “holding her up.” We weren’t given any proof of refund, only a verbal confirmation.

It’s disappointing to see a place I once really enjoyed become so poorly managed. What used to feel welcoming and efficient now feels disorganised and dismissive toward customers.

As for the gachapon machines, they feel questionable at best. My sister visited a few months ago and received the exact same keychain twice in a row—of the least popular character. Recently, I spent £6 on a broken Sanrio toy that didn’t even light up as advertised. It’s hard not to feel that the odds or quality aren’t quite as they should be.

The boba itself is decent but nothing special—certainly not enough to justify the experience or the prices.

Overall, Kissaten remains a cute and aesthetic stop in Soho, but at this point, it’s more style than substance. Sadly, it’s no longer worth the money or the frustration.

The only place I know of to get Thai tea in London. The taste is sweet and malty. It's a unique flavour and enjoyable whether hot, cold or as an ice cream.

The inside is very basic as they don't expect you to stay. If you want to sit down you'll only find a spot if it's dead quiet and the seats don't look very comfortable anyway. The service is friendly and fast so no problems there.

The cold one is quite expensive at £6 but you do get a nice reusable cup to take home.

Slightly pricy but I understand with the import cost and the cups used.

I actually brought mine home to reuse. Really enjoyed the tea, I think it’s best to drink it before the ice melts too much. As it does get quite diluted or maybe wait a little for the ice to dilute it a little and then drink it.

The flavour is delicious it is very similar to Rosa’s Thai so if you don’t have this close by that’s a pretty close alternative.

I also purchased some ready mix Thai tea mix which was just OK, I wouldn’t recommend buying it. You probably need to use 3 sachets for the same flavour or concentrate. I wish I bought the actual loose tea instead but hey there’s always a next time.

Service was good and I would try it if you’re in SoHo.

text.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Wuye Bubble Tea - A Must-Try During PandaGo's Milk Tea Festival!

Just visited Wuye Bubble Tea during PandaGo's promotion (spend £5 save £4) and it was absolutely worth it! This is hands-down one of the best bubble tea spots in London Chinatown.

🍹 MUST-TRY DRINKS:
- Passionfruit Trio – An explosion of fresh passionfruit seeds with just the right balance of tartness and sweetness. The jasmine tea base elevates the flavor, and the combination of coconut jelly, crystal boba, and tea jelly makes every sip exciting.
- Berry Braggart – Made with real crushed strawberries and peach resin (Tao Jiao), this drink is both refreshing and lightly floral. A hint of rose salt enhances the natural berry sweetness.

☕ TEA BASE HIGHLIGHTS:
- Their  Premium Black Tea has a deep, caramel-like aroma (UVA high-altitude leaves).
- The Four Seasons Green Tea is subtly floral with a touch of osmanthus—so fragrant!

🍮 DESSERT WINNER:
The Crème Brûlée  is UNREAL—thick caramelized crust with ultra-rich custard made from clotted cream. Best I’ve had in London!

⏰ Pro Tip:Go before 3 PM to avoid long waits.

Final Verdict: 10/10 for quality and flavor. Even without the discount (£5-£4 deal until March 28), it’s worth every penny!

#LondonBubbleTea #WuyeReview #PandaGoDeals #BestBubbleTeaUK

text.text:
When I placed my order, the staff member serving me did not speak a single word throughout the interaction and continuously rolled her eyes at me, which made me feel very uncomfortable. Later, when my order number was called, she handed me a straw by tossing it at me, which I found extremely offensive. Only after I asked her if she was okay did she finally say a brief “ok.”

In addition, the product I received was not up to the standard I was used to from your store. I specifically ordered my drink with less sugar, but it was extremely sweet—much sweeter than before.

I gave this experience 1 star only because I cannot give 0. I strongly believe that as the owner, you should pay more attention to your employees’ mental wellbeing. If a staff member is not ready to work, it would be better for them to stay home and rest, rather than deliver such poor service to customers.

I have always enjoyed your brand, but this experience left me very disappointed. Because of both the poor service and the unsatisfactory product, I will no longer return to this store, nor will I recommend it to my friends.

If you're ever in London's Chinatown, you absolutely have to stop by Woo Tea! I just tried their Woo Tea Watermelon Frap.
It tasted exactly like fresh, juicy watermelon – super refreshing and not too sweet. But the best part, for sure, was the tapioca. You know how some places have weird boba? Not here! Theirs was so fresh and perfectly chewy. It just made the whole drink incredible.
So yeah, if you want a really good, refreshing bubble tea with awesome boba, definitely grab the Watermelon Frap at Woo Tea. You'll love it!
